item.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta, Marina Temkin
item.description: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

item.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Phia, the shopping startup founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe, and her friend Sophia Kianni is under fire for a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, per a Bloomberg investigation.

item.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]

item.title: Oratomic raises $300M to build a viable quantum computer that needs only 20K qubits
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The massive round was co-led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, and Khosla Ventures.

item.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]

item.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

item.title: Charles Hudson shares the common mistakes he’s seen after investing in 500+ startups
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye
item.description: In this week’s episode of Build Mode, Isabelle Johannessen talks with Precursor Ventures' Charles Hudson about the headwinds facing early-stage founders today and the most common mistakes founders should avoid in order to get funded.

item.title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: A new $20 billion valuation would be a giant step up from the $10 billion valuation it reached in October.

item.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

item.title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Nilekani remains Fundamentum's anchor investor as the firm expands its leadership team and targets AI and fintech startups in India.

item.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

item.title: With EU backing, QuantumDiamonds aims to speed up chip manufacturing
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Like its U.S. counterpart, the European Chips Act aims to foster the semiconductor industry — in part thanks to state subsidies. One of the beneficiaries is QuantumDiamonds, a German startup that applies a novel approach to inspecting chips.

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time — an essential skill for producing intelligence that generalizes. That gap, it turns out, might be filled by gaming data. That’s the bet behind General Intuition, a […]

item.title: Prime Intellect raises $130M Series A to help enterprises build their own AI agents
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Founded in 2024, Prime Intellect’s goal is to give organizations capabilities to train their own agentic systems without relying on frontier AI labs.

item.title: These AI startups are growing revenue at faster and faster rates
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: There are a lot of fast-growing AI startups, but some are growing even faster, they say.

item.title: Your gaming data could be the secret to AGI, according to this Bezos-backed startup
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time — an essential skill for producing intelligence that generalizes. That gap, it turns out, might be filled by gaming data. That’s the bet behind General Intuition, a […]

item.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.
